question.text: What is the maximum profit for a short strangle on USDINR where a trader sells an 83.25 call at Rs. 0.17 and an 82.75 put at Rs. 0.12?

answer.text: Rs. 0.29 — when USDINR expires between 82.75 and 83.25, both options expire worthless
Rs. 0.17 — only the call premium since the put will be exercised
Unlimited — since the underlying can move infinitely in either direction
Rs. 0.12 — only the put premium since the call will be exercised

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Maximum profit = Total premium received = 0.17 + 0.12 = Rs. 0.29. This occurs when USDINR expires between the two strikes (82.75 to 83.25) and both options expire worthless. Outside this range, profits reduce and eventually turn to losses. **The Trap:** The short strangle has LIMITED profit but UNLIMITED loss potential. Maximum profit is the sum of BOTH premiums, not just one. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch5 covers short strangles. The strategy is identical across currency, bond, and equity options markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short strangle = selling volatility. Profitable in quiet markets but dangerous in volatile ones. Always define exit points before entering this position.
